text: The derivative of x squared with respect to x is 2x. This follows from the power rule: d/dx of x to the n equals n times x to the n minus 1.
The integral of 2x with respect to x is x squared plus a constant C. Integration is the inverse operation of differentiation.
The derivative of the natural logarithm ln x is 1 divided by x, valid for x greater than zero.
The integral of 1 over x from 1 to e equals 1, because the antiderivative is ln x and ln e minus ln 1 equals 1 minus 0.
By the chain rule, the derivative of sin of x squared is 2x times cosine of x squared.
The product rule: if f and g are differentiable, then the derivative of their product is f prime g plus f g prime.
The quadratic formula gives solutions x equals negative b plus or minus square root of b squared minus 4ac all divided by 2a.
The discriminant b squared minus 4ac determines the nature of roots: positive means two real roots, zero means one repeated root, negative means two complex roots.
By the Pythagorean theorem, in a right triangle with legs a and b and hypotenuse c, we have a squared plus b squared equals c squared.
The area of a circle with radius r is pi r squared. The circumference is 2 pi r.
Euler's formula states that e to the power of i times theta equals cosine theta plus i sine theta.
The fundamental theorem of calculus connects differentiation and integration: the definite integral of f prime from a to b equals f of b minus f of a.
A geometric series with first term a and ratio r less than 1 has sum a divided by the quantity 1 minus r.
The sum of the first n natural numbers equals n times n plus 1 divided by 2. For n equals 10, the sum is 55.
By induction: prove for n equals 1, assume for n equals k, then prove for n equals k plus 1. This establishes truth for all natural numbers.
Fermat's little theorem: if p is prime and a is not divisible by p, then a to the power p minus 1 is congruent to 1 modulo p.
The binomial theorem expands the quantity a plus b to the n as the sum from k equals 0 to n of binomial coefficient n choose k times a to the n minus k times b to the k.
The greatest common divisor of 48 and 18 is 6. The Euclidean algorithm: 48 equals 2 times 18 plus 12; 18 equals 1 times 12 plus 6; 12 equals 2 times 6 plus 0.
A prime number has exactly two positive divisors: 1 and itself. The primes below 20 are 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19.
The dot product of vectors u and v equals the magnitude of u times the magnitude of v times cosine of the angle between them.
The cross product of two vectors in three dimensions is a vector perpendicular to both, with magnitude equal to the area of the parallelogram they span.
A matrix is invertible if and only if its determinant is nonzero. The determinant of a 2 by 2 matrix with entries a, b, c, d is ad minus bc.
The eigenvalues of a matrix A satisfy the characteristic polynomial det of A minus lambda times I equals zero.
By Bayes' theorem, the posterior probability of A given B equals the likelihood of B given A times the prior of A divided by the evidence of B.
The expected value of a discrete random variable X is the sum of x times the probability that X equals x over all values x.
The variance of X equals the expected value of X squared minus the square of the expected value of X.
The normal distribution has probability density one over sigma square root 2 pi times e to the negative x minus mu squared over 2 sigma squared.
L'Hopital's rule: if the limit of f over g produces 0 over 0 or infinity over infinity, then the limit equals the limit of f prime over g prime.
Taylor series: a smooth function f can be written as the sum of f prime n at a times x minus a to the n over n factorial.
The Maclaurin series for e to the x is the sum from n equals 0 to infinity of x to the n divided by n factorial.
The sine function has Maclaurin series x minus x cubed over 3 factorial plus x to the fifth over 5 factorial minus and so on.
Partial fractions: x over the quantity x minus 1 times x plus 2 equals A over x minus 1 plus B over x plus 2. Solving gives A equals one third and B equals two thirds.
The ratio test for series convergence: if the limit of the absolute value of a n plus 1 over a n is less than 1, the series converges absolutely.
Integration by parts: the integral of u dv equals u v minus the integral of v du.
The volume of a sphere with radius r is four thirds pi r cubed. The surface area is 4 pi r squared.
By the mean value theorem, there exists a point c in the open interval a to b where f prime of c equals f of b minus f of a all divided by b minus a.
Cauchy-Schwarz inequality: the absolute value of the dot product of u and v is at most the product of their magnitudes.
The number e is irrational and transcendental, approximately equal to 2.71828182845. It is the base of the natural logarithm.
Pi is irrational and transcendental, approximately 3.14159265358. The ratio of any circle's circumference to its diameter.
The golden ratio phi equals one plus root five divided by two, approximately 1.61803. It satisfies phi squared equals phi plus one.
By the intermediate value theorem, if f is continuous on the closed interval a to b and k is between f of a and f of b, then there exists c in that interval with f of c equal to k.
The rank of a matrix plus the nullity equals the number of columns. This is the rank-nullity theorem.
Stokes' theorem relates the surface integral of the curl of a vector field to the line integral of the field over the boundary of the surface.
The divergence theorem relates the volume integral of the divergence of a vector field to the surface integral over the enclosing boundary.
A function is bijective if it is both injective (one-to-one) and surjective (onto). Bijections have well-defined inverses.
The number of ways to choose k items from n without repetition and without regard to order is n factorial divided by k factorial times n minus k factorial.
The pigeonhole principle: if n plus 1 items are placed in n containers, at least one container holds two or more items.
Modular arithmetic: 17 modulo 5 equals 2 because 17 equals 3 times 5 plus 2.
The sum of the interior angles of a polygon with n sides is n minus 2 times 180 degrees.
Complex conjugates: the product of a complex number a plus bi and its conjugate a minus bi equals a squared plus b squared, which is real.
